evidence: High fan-in hub entrypoints are concentrated in thin facades (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts`, `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts`, `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`) per packet importer counts (246, 207, 158), increasing blast radius for contract changes.

`src/shared/lib/toolIds.ts` merges two distinct namespaces (`TOOL_IDS` and `SETTINGS_IDS`) by spreading tool IDs into settings IDs, coupling tool identity and persistence keys into one shared dependency node.

`src/shared/components/ui/runtime/sonner.tsx` centralizes toast runtime through `getToastManager()` singleton access and re-exports `toast`, creating a global cross-module coordination point for notifications.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` mixes shot navigation, lightbox control, modal orchestration, and error presentation in one hook with a wide argument surface, indicating weak ownership boundaries.

`src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` combines marketing content rendering, tooltip lifecycle policy integration, navigation actions, and embedded iframe behavior in one pane component.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` is a high-responsibility shell coordinating dialog lifecycle, viewport locking, pane layout coupling, pointer/touch dismissal policy, and accessibility plumbing.

Image/lightbox edit flow passes large mutable state bags across seam boundaries: `useImageLightboxEnvironment` + `useImageLightboxSharedState` + `useImageLightboxEditing` in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` coordinate via many fields and ref handoffs rather than a narrow contract.

`useInlineEditState` assembles and re-exposes a very wide `InlineEditStateResult` contract and then `InlineEditView` forwards it into multiple subtrees, creating mapper-heavy glue (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts`, `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx`).

Task creation modules repeat similar cross-boundary choreography (validate -> normalize/build payload -> compose request -> createTask -> normalize/rethrow) in parallel implementations (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/videoEnhance.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`).
mid_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Integration seams are still dominated by broad parameter/state bags and duplicated boundary translation logic, which raises drift risk and makes changes propagate across many files.

evidence: Timer lifecycle handling is not bounded in `src/shared/components/ProductTour/index.tsx`: nested `setTimeout`/recursive polling paths are created (`pauseThenAdvance`, step-3 target wait loop) without centralized cancellation on unmount.
`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts` debounces with `frameCountDebounceRef` but does not clear pending timeout in an unmount cleanup effect.

`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts` manually mirrors a large amount of state/handlers into multiple derived objects (`inpaintingState`, `generationState`, `imageEditValue`) with duplicated field wiring and constant placeholders.
low_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Local implementations still rely on ad-hoc timer control and high-volume manual object mapping, which increases fragility and maintenance overhead.

evidence: `generateVideoService` accepts `generationMode: 'timeline' | 'batch' | 'by-pair'` but branches only on `generationMode === 'timeline'`, treating `'by-pair'` as batch behavior (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:96`, `:637-639`).

`TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` advertises a distinct `'by-pair'` API mode (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts:227`), but service orchestration does not preserve a distinct execution path.

`generateVideoService` comments that consumers should use only `generateVideo()`, but exports multiple internal builders (both `_` prefixed and non-prefixed), widening and blurring the module contract (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:567-588`).

`cropFilename` lacks `maxLength <= 0` handling unlike sibling `truncateText`, creating inconsistent contract behavior for boundary values in the same utility surface (`src/shared/lib/stringFormatting.ts:1-23`, `:25-39`).

Multiple compatibility alias modules remain in active use instead of being fully retired: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` exposes only deprecated pass-through wrappers and triggers deprecation enforcement at import time (`handleError`/`handleAndRethrow` with `@removalTarget 2026-06-30`).
`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` is a deprecated pure re-export shim, indicating ongoing migration lag rather than a stable API layer.

Canonical travel types still embed legacy compatibility surface (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/types.ts` has both 'NEW unified structure guidance' and legacy UI fields), while `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` retains broad deprecated field support.
`src/shared/types/referenceImage.ts` still carries legacy migration fields with a non-enforced note ('will be removed after bulk migration'), prolonging mixed-shape data contracts.

The deprecated shim `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` is only a re-export to `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`, keeping a legacy path alive in the wrong package boundary.
`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` imports from `@/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState`, so a feature-owned hook test is physically located in shared hooks tests.

Holistic structure shows `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` has 74 files and imports mostly from `src/shared/hooks/` (73 edges) but also from `src/features/gallery/hooks/` (1 edge), indicating cross-boundary leakage in a high-volume test folder.

`src/shared/lib/__tests__/clientThumbnailGenerator.test.ts` tests `@/shared/media/clientThumbnailGenerator` while living under `shared/lib/__tests__`, and reaches `shared/lib/imageUploader` via `../imageUploader`, mixing media-vs-lib ownership.

Supabase edge handlers repeatedly inline the same request bootstrap/auth/method/body flow (e.g., get-task-output lines 30-82, get-completed-segments lines 25-73, get-orchestrator-children lines 30-82) instead of sharing one endpoint composition surface.

Runtime composition is inconsistent across sibling edge functions: setup-auto-topup uses shared initEdgeRuntime/jsonResponse utilities (lines 4-10, 43-61), while stripe-checkout defines a local jsonResponse helper and manual env/client bootstrapping (lines 13-24, 83-89).

Timeline interaction hooks expose wide 'context-bag' interfaces and blend multiple behaviors in one unit (useTimelineActionHandlers input lines 14-57 plus drop/duplicate/tap/video-browser logic lines 124-220; useShotImagesEditorCallbacks combines DB mutations + cache invalidation + UI callback wiring lines 56-195, 197-260).

Timeline write orchestration patterns are duplicated across hooks: both useTimelineCore and useTimelineFrameUpdates define near-identical logging helpers (`shortId`, queue phase logger) and queue-wrapped write flows.

In src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts, most failure paths use normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow, but createGeneration throws a raw Error for the first insert failure (`if (error || !data) throw new Error(...)`).

src/shared/lib/governance/deprecationEnforcement.ts uses `throw new Error` and `console.error` directly for deprecation signaling, while the app-wide error path is centered on runtimeError/presentationReporter normalization.
src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts reports circuit-breaker failures via direct `toast.error(...)` instead of the shared presentationReporter/runtimeError path used elsewhere.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Related runtime paths still mix normalized AppError reporting with raw throw/log/toast behavior, so failure handling remains partially inconsistent across adjacent modules.

findings.summary: Tool identity and settings identity are co-located in one shared hub, coupling unrelated modules through a single contract.
evidence: `SETTINGS_IDS` is defined as `{ ...TOOL_IDS, ... }`, so tool IDs are treated as settings IDs in the same module.
The module also exports both validators (`isToolId`, `isKnownSettingsId`), encouraging broad import usage for distinct concerns.
This creates cross-module dependency pressure on a single file for both routing/tool semantics and settings persistence semantics.
findings.suggestion: Split into separate bounded modules (`toolIds.ts` and `settingsIds.ts`) with an explicit mapping layer only where overlap is intended; migrate call sites to import only the boundary they need.

findings.summary: Notification handling relies on a global runtime singleton, creating hidden cross-module coupling.
evidence: `Toaster` binds to `getToastManager()` and the module re-exports a global `toast` API.
Error pathways in gallery handlers already depend on centralized runtime error normalization, making user-feedback behavior implicitly coupled to shared global notification runtime.
This pattern increases risk of side effects and ordering assumptions across modules that call into shared error/notification paths.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an injected notification interface at feature boundaries (or React context-backed facade), so feature hooks depend on a contract instead of global singleton runtime objects.

findings.summary: Media gallery orchestration mixes multiple responsibilities in hooks/components that should be split by ownership boundary.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryHandlers` coordinates navigation, lightbox closing, shot switching, task-details modal state, and error reporting in one unit.
`useMediaGalleryItemState` owns local UI state plus async shot creation workflow plus error presentation.
`ShotActions` receives deeply nested state/action objects (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`), indicating responsibility aggregation rather than clear sub-feature boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Refactor into smaller feature-specific controllers (navigation controller, shot-association controller, details-modal controller) and keep UI state hooks focused on local state only.

findings.summary: Top-level UI shells accumulate policy and infrastructure concerns, blurring component ownership.
evidence: `LightboxShell` handles dialog state interception (`eventDetails.cancel()`), pointer/touch policy, viewport layout, and accessibility all in one shell.
`CreativePartnerPane` combines static narrative content, tooltip interaction policy, navigation routing, and external embed concerns in a single component.
`InstallInstructionsModal` mixes scenario resolution, modal composition, and fallback navigation behavior directly in presentation code.
findings.suggestion: Move interaction and policy logic into dedicated view-model hooks/services, and keep shell components focused on rendering and composition of explicit child modules.

findings.summary: Edit/lightbox seams rely on oversized state bags and ref-mediated handoffs instead of narrow contracts.
evidence: `ImageLightbox` creates `isInpaintModeRef`/`isMagicEditModeRef` in shared-state setup and passes their `.current` values into `useSharedLightboxState` before writing updated values later in `useImageLightboxEditing` (`ImageLightbox.tsx` lines 226-279, 358-359).

`useInlineEditState` constructs large mapped contracts (`buildInlineEditStateModels`) and then returns another broad contract (`InlineEditStateResult`), with downstream forwarding in `InlineEditView` into `EditModePanel` and canvas controls.
`LightboxLayout` conditionally renders core UI behavior from these propagated mode flags (`isSpecialEditMode`, edit mode), so stale or over-broad seam state directly affects layout behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split edit/lightbox seams into focused view-model contracts per concern (navigation, variant ops, edit-mode, render-only media state). Replace ref-mediated mode handoff with explicit state derived once in the orchestrator and passed as immutable snapshot props/context per render.

findings.summary: Task modules duplicate the same boundary choreography, increasing drift between tools.
evidence: Each module repeats near-identical flow: validate input, build task params/payload, `createTask(composeTaskRequest(...))`, then `rethrowTaskCreationError(...)` on failure.
Batch and single-task paths are implemented separately in multiple files (`imageGeneration.ts`, `magicEdit.ts`) with similar stage sequencing but different ad-hoc naming/shape conventions.
Cross-tool payload construction logic is distributed across separate modules, making seam behavior inconsistent to audit.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared `createCanonicalTask` pipeline utility with pluggable stages (`validate`, `resolveDefaults`, `buildParams`, `postCreate`) and migrate tool modules to declarative per-tool adapters.

findings.summary: Multiple `setTimeout` flows are created without robust teardown, risking stale state updates.
evidence: `ProductTour` uses `pauseThenAdvance` and additional nested timers in joyride callback (including recursive `waitForTarget`) but does not track/cancel all outstanding timers during unmount or run-state changes.
`useSegmentSlotMode` sets `frameCountDebounceRef.current = setTimeout(...)` for frame updates, but there is no cleanup effect to clear the pending timeout when the hook unmounts.
findings.suggestion: Centralize timer management with refs plus a single cleanup effect (`return () => clearTimeout(...)` for every active timer). For recursive polling, store a cancellation flag/ref and stop scheduling once unmounted or when run conditions change.

findings.summary: Inline edit state is manually mirrored into multiple large objects, creating fragile local wiring.
evidence: `buildInlineEditStateModels` maps dozens of fields from hook outputs into `inpaintingState`, `generationState`, and `imageEditValue`, repeating many references and setters.

The returned API includes placeholder constants (`isFlippedHorizontally: false`, `isSaving: false`) in `imageEditValue`, which makes the local contract broader than actual behavior and easier to misuse.
`InlineEditView` consumes this broad shape and forwards it wholesale to `ImageEditProvider` and `EditModePanel`, amplifying coupling to field-by-field wiring.
findings.suggestion: Replace manual mirroring with smaller typed selectors/factories per consumer (`canvasModel`, `panelModel`, `providerModel`) and remove non-functional placeholder fields from exposed state contracts.

findings.summary: `by-pair` is exposed as a first-class API mode but executed as batch, creating contract/behavior drift.
evidence: `GenerateVideoParams` includes `'by-pair'` (`generateVideoService.ts:96`) and request body forwards `generation_mode` unchanged (`:553`).
Pair config selection only distinguishes `timeline` vs non-`timeline` (`:637-639`), so `'by-pair'` silently takes batch config path.
Task param type explicitly documents `'by-pair'` as a separate mode (`types.ts:227`), implying dedicated semantics that are not honored in orchestration.
findings.suggestion: Make mode handling explicit: either implement a true `by-pair` branch with distinct pairConfig/build rules, or remove `'by-pair'` from public unions until behavior exists. Add contract tests that assert mode-specific payload differences.

findings.summary: The service advertises a single public API but exports many internal builders, creating an unstable and inconsistent module surface.
evidence: Comment says consumers should use `generateVideo()` as the public API (`generateVideoService.ts:569-571`).
Module still exports multiple internals, including non-test-prefixed helpers (`:580-587`), making contract boundaries ambiguous.
These exported internals are tightly coupled to large request-shape types from travel task contracts, increasing blast radius for future API changes.
findings.suggestion: Reduce exports to a stable minimal surface (`generateVideo` + required types). Move test-only helpers to a dedicated internal module imported by tests, or gate with an explicit internal namespace to prevent accidental production coupling.

findings.summary: Abort semantics are accepted at the API boundary but not propagated through the async fetch seam, so canceled callers still wait on in-flight work.
evidence: `fetchToolSettingsSupabase(..., signal?)` checks `signal.aborted` before start (`toolSettingsService.ts:358-363`) and once before querying (`:380-384`), but does not pass `signal` into `fetchAllScopes` calls (`:386-389`).
If an identical request is already in-flight, the function awaits it unconditionally (`:367-370`) without re-checking caller cancellation.
A contrasting module (`timelineWriteQueue`) uses abort-aware execution with timeout and explicit abort handling (`timelineWriteQueue.ts:54-84`), highlighting inconsistency at integration seams.
findings.suggestion: Honor cancellation end-to-end: thread `AbortSignal` through scope fetches (or race with an abort promise), and short-circuit coalesced callers on abort while leaving shared work for non-aborted callers.

findings.summary: One malformed pending clip invalidates and clears the entire queue, dropping otherwise valid clip actions.
evidence: `parsePendingJoinClips` returns `null` immediately on first invalid item (`clipInitService.ts:155-169`), not a partial valid subset.
`consumePendingJoinClips` treats `null` as invalid payload, logs failure, removes storage key, and returns failure (`:203-217`, `:250`).
This makes localStorage handoff brittle: a single corrupted record can discard all pending user-intended clips in that batch.
findings.suggestion: Parse defensively per entry: keep valid items, record invalid-count diagnostics, and only clear/drop malformed records. Return success with surviving actions plus a degraded warning code when partial recovery occurs.

findings.summary: `cropFilename` does not enforce boundary behavior for non-positive limits, unlike sibling truncation API.
evidence: `truncateText` explicitly handles `maxLength <= 0` by returning empty string (`stringFormatting.ts:26-28`).
`cropFilename` has no equivalent guard (`:1-23`); for negative values it can return strings longer than the requested max, violating implied `maxLength` contract.
findings.suggestion: Align boundary semantics across formatting helpers by adding an early `maxLength <= 0` branch (and a small test matrix for 0/negative values).

findings.summary: Deprecated pass-through alias modules are still part of the active import surface, extending migration debt and runtime deprecation signaling.
evidence: `handleError.ts` only forwards to runtimeError helpers and marks both exports deprecated with a removal target, yet remains as an import path with deprecation enforcement side effects.
`useGenerationMutations.ts` is a deprecated one-line re-export shim with no additional logic.
`toolConstants.ts` is another deprecated compatibility shim re-exporting canonical `toolIds/toolRoutes` and signaling migration on import.
findings.suggestion: Set a hard sunset plan: migrate remaining importers to canonical modules, add lint/no-restricted-import rules for these alias paths, then delete alias files in one coordinated refactor.

findings.summary: Legacy and modern payload shapes coexist in core travel/reference contracts, keeping migration complexity in primary domain types.
evidence: `types.ts` introduces 'NEW unified structure guidance' but still includes `StructureVideoLegacyUiFields` inside `StructureVideoConfigWithMetadata`.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` retains a wide deprecated field matrix plus policy collection/enforcement helpers, indicating a still-open compatibility boundary.
`referenceImage.ts` includes legacy persisted fields alongside current settings fields, with only a comment-based migration intent.
findings.suggestion: Separate legacy read-shapes into adapter-only modules and keep canonical interfaces legacy-free; add explicit conversion at boundaries and enforce canonical write-shape in shared APIs.

findings.summary: Join-clips compat adapter remains a large mixed-mode translator despite canonical API availability, signaling unfinished migration cleanup.
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` keeps extensive legacy parameter handling (`mode: 'legacy'`, legacy path fields, legacy video-edit payload fields) while also mapping to `createCanonicalJoinClipsTask`.
The adapter throws/branches on multiple legacy paths and removal-target checks, indicating the module is still carrying historical protocol complexity rather than test-only minimal compatibility.
findings.suggestion: Narrow this adapter to a strictly test-fixture utility (or remove it), and move any unavoidable compatibility conversion into a small boundary mapper with a single accepted legacy input shape.

findings.summary: Shot editor state types contain stale action variants for a removed state field, reducing local contract clarity.
evidence: `ShotEditorState` explicitly notes `localOrderedShotImages` was removed, but `ShotEditorAction` still declares `'SET_LOCAL_ORDERED_SHOT_IMAGES'` with `GenerationRow[]` payload.
Because `state/index.ts` re-exports all types, this stale action remains part of the public state contract.
findings.suggestion: Remove the stale action type and any dead dispatch/reducer handling for it, then let TypeScript exhaustiveness checks validate remaining state transitions.

findings.summary: Deprecated shared hook shim preserves a stale package boundary for generation mutations.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` contains only a deprecated re-export of domain hook symbols.
Search for `@/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations` in `src/` returns no active imports, indicating the compatibility file is now residue.
Downstream tests and feature hooks already reference domain paths directly (`@/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations`).
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) target folder: keep canonical mutations in `src/domains/generation/hooks/` only; (2) move order: first update any remaining imports to `@/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations`, then remove `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`; (3) import-update/validation commands: `rg -n "@/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations" src`, apply replacements, then run `pnpm test -w 1 src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.summary: Gallery feature hook test is stored in shared hooks test package, obscuring ownership.
evidence: `useGalleryPageState.test.ts` imports the hook from `@/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState` while residing under `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/`.
The feature already has a dedicated test folder (`src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/`) used by `useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts`.
Holistic coupling records `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/ -> src/features/gallery/hooks/` edge, a placement mismatch against dominant shared-hook mirroring.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) target folder: `src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/`; (2) move order: move `useGalleryPageState.test.ts` first, then normalize its imports to local feature paths; (3) import-update/validation commands: `git mv src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts`, then `rg -n "useGalleryPageState" src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__`, run `pnpm test -w 1 src/features/gallery/hooks/__tests__/useGalleryPageState.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.summary: Client thumbnail test location blurs the boundary between media capability and generic lib utilities.
evidence: The test file lives under `src/shared/lib/__tests__/` but its primary unit under test is `@/shared/media/clientThumbnailGenerator`.
Within that same test, uploader mocks are imported via `../imageUploader`, coupling the test to lib internals while validating media behavior.
This split forces cross-package reasoning for one capability (thumbnail generation + upload), reducing top-level role clarity.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) target folders: move the test to `src/shared/media/__tests__/` and keep storage-upload contract tests in `src/shared/lib/__tests__/`; (2) move order: relocate `clientThumbnailGenerator.test.ts`, then switch mocked importer path to `@/shared/lib/imageUploader`; (3) import-update/validation commands: `mkdir -p src/shared/media/__tests__ && git mv src/shared/lib/__tests__/clientThumbnailGenerator.test.ts src/shared/media/__tests__/clientThumbnailGenerator.test.ts`, then `rg -n "clientThumbnailGenerator|imageUploader" src/shared/media/__tests__`, run `pnpm test -w 1 src/shared/media/__tests__/clientThumbnailGenerator.test.ts` and `pnpm typecheck`.

findings.summary: Edge endpoint setup/auth flow is duplicated across many handlers with no single composition point.
evidence: Each file repeats the same structural sequence: env lookup -> createClient -> logger -> method guard -> parse body -> authenticateRequest -> ownership checks (e.g., get-task-output lines 30-82; get-completed-segments lines 25-73; get-orchestrator-children lines 30-82).
The repeated structure already varies in details (`req.text()+JSON.parse` vs `req.json()`), showing drift inside supposed common behavior.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared edge endpoint wrapper in `_shared` (e.g., `withEdgeHandler`) that standardizes runtime init, method validation, body parsing mode, auth policy, and error-to-response mapping; migrate these handlers to pass only endpoint-specific business logic.

findings.summary: Sibling edge functions use conflicting runtime abstractions, causing pattern fragmentation.
evidence: stripe-checkout defines its own `jsonResponse` and manual bootstrapping (lines 13-24, 83-89), while setup-auto-topup/complete-auto-topup-setup/generate-pat use shared `jsonResponse` + `initEdgeRuntime` + strict parser utilities (setup-auto-topup lines 4-10, 43-61).
process-auto-topup also defines local CORS/json helpers (lines 7-18), creating multiple competing patterns for the same concern set.
findings.suggestion: Unify all edge functions on one runtime stack (`initEdgeRuntime` + shared response helpers + shared parse/auth helpers), then remove local helper implementations to enforce one extension path for new endpoints.

findings.summary: Timeline callback hooks are overloaded with mixed responsibilities and oversized context bags.
evidence: useTimelineActionHandlers takes a 30+ field input object (lines 14-57) and then handles unrelated concerns in one hook: drop interception, duplicate frame logic, tap-to-move, endpoint dragging, and structure-video insertion (lines 124-220).
useShotImagesEditorCallbacks combines UI callback wiring with direct Supabase reads/deletes and broad cache invalidation orchestration inside the same callback layer (lines 56-195, 197-260).
useTimelineViewportController also follows a wide input contract pattern (lines 12-26), reinforcing 'pass-everything-in' orchestration rather than narrow feature-level collaborators.
findings.suggestion: Split timeline hooks by concern (drop pipeline, frame math, structure-video placement, persistence/cache effects) and replace wide input bags with small typed collaborator objects per concern; keep top-level hooks as thin composition layers only.

findings.summary: Timeline write-queue orchestration is duplicated across two hooks instead of centralized.
evidence: Both files define near-identical queue logging scaffolding (`shortId` + `logQueuePhase`) and console-prefix machinery (useTimelineCore lines 36-41 and 193-231; useTimelineFrameUpdates lines 40-45 and 93-143).
Both wrap DB writes through serialized queue + timeout patterns with similar operational concerns, but implemented separately, increasing drift risk when changing queue behavior or observability.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared timeline write-orchestration utility (logging, timeout hooks, queue phase callbacks, cache refetch policy) and have both hooks call that utility instead of maintaining parallel orchestration logic.

findings.summary: Large UI modules still mix environment assembly, workflow orchestration, and rendering concerns.
evidence: ImageLightbox is 887 LOC and combines runtime/environment derivation, feature orchestration, and view composition in one file despite partial internal split (props and environment wiring begin lines 67-187, then additional orchestration and render layers continue across file).
GenerationsPane (621 LOC) combines pane layout/state machine, routing behavior, event-bus listeners, filter model, and gallery rendering in one component (lines 45-238 shown before markup body).
TaskDetailsModal mixes modal state control, async task-id/task fetch orchestration, clipboard side effects, and details rendering in one component (lines 45-141 plus render tree).
findings.suggestion: Adopt a stricter presenter/container split: move async orchestration and state machines into dedicated hooks/services, keep components focused on rendering and local interaction state; enforce file-level role boundaries in this subsystem.

findings.summary: createGeneration uses raw Error throw in a module otherwise standardized on runtime normalization
evidence: useGenerationMutations.ts createGeneration does `throw new Error(`Failed to create generation: ...`)` when insert fails, while nearby scope/variant failures use normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow with context/logData.
This creates two different error shapes and context propagation paths inside one mutation module.
findings.suggestion: Replace the raw throw branch in createGeneration with normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow(error ?? new Error('Failed to create generation'), { context: 'useCreateGeneration.createGeneration', showToast: false, logData: { projectId: params.projectId } }) so this path matches the module’s existing contract.

findings.summary: Deprecation enforcement emits raw throw/console paths instead of shared error reporter
evidence: signalPastRemovalTargetUsage throws `new Error(message)` for throw mode and uses `console.error(message)` for warn mode.
handleError.ts invokes signalPastRemovalTargetUsage at module load, so behavior can become import-time throw/log outside the normalized AppError contracts.
findings.suggestion: Route deprecation signaling through a single reporting boundary (e.g., use runtimeError/presentationReporter with `showToast: false` and explicit deprecation metadata), and reserve throw mode for explicitly opted-in call sites rather than import-time side effects.

findings.summary: Realtime fetch-failure UI errors are surfaced via direct toast without normalized error context
evidence: DataFreshnessManager.onFetchFailure computes error type and calls toast.error(userMessage, ...) directly when breaker trips.
The same class uses normalizeAndPresentError in notifySubscribers catch path, so error presentation strategy differs within one component.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a small adapter in DataFreshnessManager that calls presentError/normalizeAndPresentError with a synthetic contextual error (including query key, failure count, classified type), and keep toast text as presentation metadata rather than a separate direct path.
